hero.kicker: Applied AI / fintech operations / automation infrastructure

hero.subheadline: My work sits at the intersection of internal platforms, backend services, and control surfaces that need reliability, visibility, and execution discipline in production.
hero.support: Strongest signal: applied AI pipelines, fintech operations tooling, and long-running automation services built for actual operators.

flagshipProjects.name: AION NEXUS
flagshipProjects.summary: Macro-intelligence pipeline that collects geopolitical, economic, and market data, normalizes it into typed models, and translates it into structured downstream risk analysis.
flagshipProjects.role: System design, connector architecture, inference workflow design, and delivery pipeline ownership.
highlights: Parallel connectors for GDELT, FRED, SGS/BCB, and market data feeds.
Typed normalization before model inference and alert generation.
Structured reasoning focused on macro and credit-risk spillover.

media.summary: Parallel source ingestion, typed analysis, and downstream risk alerts for credit-sensitive workflows.

flagshipProjects.name: OWN Acquirer Panel
flagshipProjects.summary: Operator panel for acquiring workflows covering transactions, settlements, accreditation, protocols, diagnostics, and webhook-aware support flows.
flagshipProjects.role: Platform architecture, integration surface design, and operator dashboard ownership.
highlights: Modular API surface for payments operations and reconciliation.
Chunked sync strategy designed to protect upstream provider APIs.
Audit, correlation, auth, and webhook validation in the service layer.
Dashboard workflows built for operators rather than generic admin UI.

media.summary: Actual acquiring operations dashboard with settlements, monitoring, support routing, and payment workflow control.

flagshipProjects.name: LAW FIDC Command Center
flagshipProjects.summary: Research and operations platform for receivables workflows combining AI routing, CRM-style process management, newsroom analysis, async jobs, and internal control surfaces.
flagshipProjects.role: Architecture direction, backend platform design, and AI workflow integration.
highlights: Next.js and FastAPI architecture with Postgres, Redis, and Celery.
Task-aware model routing instead of a single-model dependency.
RBAC, structured logs, and audit-friendly workflow tables.
Business-process depth across research, tasks, templates, and AI analysis.

media.summary: Receivables operations platform spanning research, AI routing, task flow, and background execution control.

domains.title: AI Systems

domains.title: Automation Platforms
domains.summary: Long-running services, schedulers, jobs, and runtime helpers designed for repeatability and unattended execution.

domains.summary: Service boundaries, typed request models, orchestration layers, and workflow-aware APIs built for maintainability.

philosophy.title: Operator-grade interfaces
philosophy.summary: Dashboards and control panels should help teams run workflows, inspect state, and resolve issues instead of acting as decorative reporting layers.

philosophy.title: Separation of concerns
philosophy.summary: Frontend surfaces, APIs, schedulers, integrations, and storage are easier to evolve when each layer has a clear responsibility.

philosophy.title: Typed integrations
philosophy.summary: External APIs become safer and easier to reason about once they are normalized into typed models before they hit business logic or model prompts.

philosophy.title: Audit-safe workflows
philosophy.summary: Request IDs, workflow logs, structured errors, and persistent run state matter whenever operators need to trust what the system just did.

philosophy.title: Runtime discipline
philosophy.summary: Schedulers, retries, background jobs, deploy scripts, and health checks are part of the product quality bar, not post-launch cleanup.

caseStudies.context: AION NEXUS is built for environments where raw geopolitical and macroeconomic events need to be translated into concrete downstream credit risk and operator-facing signal.
caseStudies.problem: A model call alone does not solve that problem. The hard part is collecting the right sources, normalizing them, shaping a disciplined context, and delivering the result in a usable workflow.
caseStudies.architecture: The system uses async connectors for multiple external feeds, typed normalization through Pydantic models, a structured inference engine, and alert delivery through observer-style outputs.
decisions: Keep the connector layer parallel and retry-aware to reduce latency without sacrificing stability.
Normalize source data before inference so the model works from structure rather than raw feed noise.
Treat delivery as part of the architecture so the analysis becomes operational instead of remaining trapped in a terminal.
caseStudies.outcome: The result is an applied AI system that shows strong engineering maturity: models sit inside a reliable pipeline with clear interfaces, typed state, and operator value.

caseStudies.context: OWN Acquirer Panel targets the operational reality of payments teams that need a unified control surface for transactions, settlement state, webhooks, diagnostics, and support flows.
caseStudies.problem: Without an internal platform, payment operations get fragmented across vendor portals, manual exports, and disconnected support processes.
caseStudies.architecture: The platform is organized as a monorepo with a modular Express API, Prisma-backed persistence, sync orchestration, webhook handlers, and a Next.js dashboard focused on operator workflows.
decisions: Chunk sync windows to reduce load on upstream APIs and make retries safer.
Keep auth, request correlation, audit handling, and webhook validation close to the service boundary.
Build the UI as a control room for operators instead of a generic admin shell.
caseStudies.outcome: This project shows credible fintech systems thinking: explicit state, provider-aware sync logic, operational tooling, and interfaces built for day-to-day production use.

caseStudies.context: LAW FIDC Command Center is designed for research-heavy and workflow-heavy receivables operations where AI, process management, and internal controls need to coexist.
caseStudies.problem: AI-enhanced internal platforms become brittle quickly when prompts, permissions, async jobs, and workflow state are scattered across disconnected tools or scripts.
caseStudies.architecture: The platform combines a Next.js frontend with FastAPI services, Postgres, Redis, Celery, and a provider-aware AI layer so research, newsroom analysis, tasks, and process state live inside explicit application boundaries.
decisions: Route model usage by task and responsibility rather than locking the platform to one provider path.
Bake RBAC, structured logs, and auditable state into the system from the start.
Treat AI as one layer inside a larger platform architecture rather than as the entire product.
caseStudies.outcome: The outcome is a system that reads as real platform engineering: workflow depth, AI integration, async execution, and operational maintainability in one coherent stack.
